chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْعِلَاجِ لِلْمُحْرِمِ إِذَا مَرِضَ أَوْ أَصَابَهُ جُرْحٌ أَوْ خُرَاجٌ أَوْ عِلَّةٌ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 94 – The treatment for the one in Ihraam when he falls sick, or is hit by an injury, or pus, or a disease

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/318)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/318)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الْمُحْرِمِ يَعْصِرُ الدُّمَّلَ وَ يَرْبِطُ عَلَى الْقَرْحَةِ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Muawiya Bin Ammar,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws, said, ‘I asked himasws about the one in Ihraam squeezing the pimple and bandage upon his ulcer. Heasws said: ‘There is no problem’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Um ay r from Mu‘awiyah ibn ‘Ammar who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who is in the state of Ihram; if he can squeeze a pimple or bandage a wound. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not harmful.’”
